mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust aggregate demand without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An index of industrial production above its 12-month moving average reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO above its moving average coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations during recovery periods, or predictive power vanishes when GDP growth is negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 2.5%, borrowing costs for corporations and consumers rise sufficiently to dampen earnings growth expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, or the threshold effect disappears in a regime-switching model.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day SPX returns, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and real GDP growth signal strengthening consumer confidence and durable goods demand, creating a positive feedback loop that drives S&P 500 returns higher over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative SPX returns when HOUST declines quarter-over-quarter, or the interaction effect is overwhelmed by monetary policy shocks in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in industrial production exceeding 0.5% indicates immediate supply-side bottlenecks and weakening corporate output, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances following a 0.5% INDPRO decline, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when adjusted for seasonal factors.
